title: Question of Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, and Lopes (2023)
statement_text: Is large clique number in tournaments always certified by a bounded-size set? Specifically, if $T$ is a tournament of sufficiently large clique number $\overrightarrow{\omega}(T)$, must $T$ contain a subtournament of large clique number belonging to one of two simple families of tournaments?
context_text: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, and Lopes (2023) introduced the clique number $\overrightarrow{\omega}(T)$ of a tournament $T$, defined as $\min_{B}\omega(B)$ over all backedge graphs $B$ of $T$. They asked whether, analogously to clique number in undirected graphs, large values of $\overrightarrow{\omega}(T)$ are always witnessed by a simple, bounded-size subtournament structure. This question also connects to a line of research by Kim and Kim (2018) on unavoidable subtournaments in tournaments with large dichromatic number.
paper_contribution: Proves that if $T$ is a tournament of sufficiently large clique number, then $T$ contains a subtournament of large clique number from one of two simple families of tournaments, thereby confirming that large clique number is always certified by a bounded-size set and fully answering the question.
notes: The verbatim statement of the original question from [original] (Aboulker et al. 2023) is not reproduced in the provided paper content; the statement_text is reconstructed from the abstract. Confidence is medium accordingly.
paper_title: Characterizing Large Clique Number in Tournaments
